title: Token-to-Word Ratios
concept: A lower token-to-word ratio (closer to 1.0) means the model can process more text within its fixed context window, saving compute.

exercises.prompt: What does the 'token-to-word ratio' tell us about an AI?

options.text: How many tokens the AI uses compared to the number of words.
How fast the AI can type words on the screen.
The number of pictures the AI can generate per second.

exercises.explain: Tokens are the basic building blocks (like words or sub-words) that an AI reads. Words are the standard dictionary terms humans use. The ratio compares the two.

cards.title: The Secret to AI Memory
cards.body: Have you ever wondered why some AI models can read entire novels, while others forget what you said ten minutes ago? It all comes down to how efficiently they chop up words!

cards.title: What is a Token-to-Word Ratio?
cards.body: The token-to-word ratio measures how many pieces (tokens) the AI splits a word into. A ratio of 1.0 is the goal: it means 1 word equals 1 token. Lower ratios mean the AI is highly efficient, processing more actual text inside its limited memory window.

cards.title: The Suitcase Packing Analogy
cards.body: Imagine packing a suitcase. If you pack whole outfits together, you fit a lot more clothes (low ratio). If you cut every shirt and pant into tiny shreds of fabric before packing, your suitcase fills up with tiny scraps and you barely fit anything useful (high ratio).

cards.title: Splitting the Word 'unbelievable'
cards.body:
Compare two tokenizers on the word 'unbelievable':

• Tokenizer A splits it into: [un, believable] (2 tokens, ratio 2.0)
• Tokenizer B splits it into: [u, n, b, e, l, i, e, v, a, b, l, e] (12 tokens, ratio 12.0)

Tokenizer A uses way less space to store the exact same word!

cards.title: Is More Always Better?
cards.body: People think that a higher number of tokens is always better because it sounds like 'more'. In reality, more tokens per word just wastes space. A lower ratio (closer to 1.0) is much better because it saves precious memory and compute power.

cards.title: Visualizing the Ratio
cards.body: See how the same phrase looks to two different AI systems. A lower ratio means cleaner, more efficient processing!

exercises.prompt: Which token-to-word ratio is more efficient for an AI model to process?

options.text: A ratio of 4.5, because more tokens are always better.
A ratio of 1.1, because it uses fewer tokens to represent the same text.
A ratio of 10.0, because it breaks words into single letters.
Both ratios perform exactly the same.

exercises.explain: A ratio of 1.1 means almost every word is kept as a single token, which is highly efficient. A ratio of 4.5 means words are broken into many small, wasteful fragments.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A lower token-to-word ratio allows a model to process more text within its fixed context window.
exercises.explain: A lower ratio means fewer tokens are used per word, allowing more text to fit into the AI's limited context window.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to show the benefit of a low token-to-word ratio:
exercises.sentence: A lower ratio uses fewer tokens, which ___ memory space.
exercises.explain: A lower token-to-word ratio means the AI uses fewer tokens for the same amount of text, which saves precious space in its memory window.

exercises.prompt: Identify which AI system is optimized to fit the most text into its context window:

options.text: AI System A: A language model with a token-to-word ratio of 1.1.
AI System B: A language model with a token-to-word ratio of 4.8.
AI System C: A model that converts every letter into three separate symbols.

exercises.explain: AI System A is highly efficient because it keeps whole words intact (ratio 1.1), while AI System B chops everything into tiny, inefficient fragments.

exercises.prompt: Why does a lower token-to-word ratio (closer to 1.0) help an AI model process larger books or documents?

options.text: It lets the AI process more text because its memory window isn't filled by tiny word fragments.
It makes the AI run slower because whole words are harder to read.
It requires the AI to use more computer power for every single character.
It forces the AI to forget the beginning of the conversation faster.

exercises.explain: A low token-to-word ratio means words are kept whole, taking up fewer slots in the model's limited memory window. This allows it to read and write much longer documents at once.
